title: Boat Rental Costs in Reykjavik 2026: Prices and Tips
html:
Planning a voyage from the Old Harbour in Reykjavik typically costs between 120 and 3,500 euros per half-day, depending on the boat type and season. Whether you want to explore Faxaflói Bay or head to Viðey Island, BoatHire24 offers transparent listings so you can rent a boat in Reykjavik with confidence.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Reykjavik?

Prices range from 120 euros for a shared whale-watching tour to over 3,500 euros for a crewed luxury motor yacht. The exact figure depends on boat size, duration, and whether you book a skippered or bareboat charter. Most operators in the Old Harbour offer half-day (4-hour) and full-day (8-hour) hire, with fuel and VAT typically included in the advertised rate. You can rent a boat in a few minutes.

  • Shared whale-watching tour: from 120 euros per person, 3 hours
  • Small motorboat (up to 6 guests, half-day): from 450 euros
  • Catamaran (up to 12 guests, full-day): from 1,200 euros
  • Crewwed yacht (up to 8 guests, full-day): from 2,800 euros

Where to set off in Reykjavik

Most departures happen from the Old Harbour (Miðbakki pier), which shelters fishing boats and charter vessels. Nearby, the Sundahöfn commercial port handles larger yachts, while private slips exist at Kópavogur, about 10 minutes south. To the west, the Hafnarfjörður mariner offers another gateway, and Akurey Island sits eight nautical miles north of the harbour, a popular stop for puffin colonies. One option worth a look is the Mefasa 90 for hire.

Local skippers often recommend sailing towards the Reykjanes peninsula, where you might spot surfacing whales. The distance from Reykjavik to the mid-Faxaflói feeding grounds is roughly 12 nautical miles, a comfortable run for a half-day trip.

Which boat to rent

Your choice should match the experience you want: wildlife, relaxation, or speed. Below are the main types available through the marketplace, where you can also check how boat rental works before booking. You can also see more at check availability.

  1. Axopar or similar cabin cruiser, best for coastal touring, up to 8 guests, from 600 euros half-day
  2. Hardtop motor yacht (e.g. the Mefasa 90 for hire), best for comfortable day-charters in variable weather, up to 10 guests, from 1,400 euros half-day
  3. Lagoon catamaran, best for stable island-hopping, up to 12 guests, from 1,200 euros half-day
  4. Rigid inflatable (RIB), best for fast wildlife excursions, up to 6 guests, from 350 euros half-day

Another popular choice is the zodiac-style RIB, ideal for photography trips around the coast. Each listing on the site details safety equipment, fuel policies, and cancellation terms. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Reykjavik?

Iceland requires no formal licence for pleasure craft under 10 metres, though rental operators will ask for a valid skipper certificate or substantial experience. Most charter firms provide a professional skipper as part of the fee, and this is mandatory for larger yachts. Always check the individual listing; the operator confirms exact requirements on their BoatHire24 page.

During the June-to-August peak, expect 24-hour daylight and frequent winds from the south-east. An extra layer and a windproof jacket are worth packing, even in midsummer.

At a glance

  • Price band: 120, 3,500 euros
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 3 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: euros (ISK also accepted domestically)

Questions about boat rental in Reykjavik

Can I rent a boat for a single day in Reykjavik?

Yes, most operators offer half-day and full-day rentals from May to September, with some available year-round for whale watching.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip in Reykjavik?

High season runs from June to August, when the weather is mildest and daylight lasts over 20 hours, though the south-east wind can still pick up.

Are there any age restrictions for driving a rental boat?

You must be at least 18 years old to skipper a bareboat charter, though passengers of all ages are welcome on crewed trips.

Do I need to pay a deposit for a charter in Reykjavik?

A security deposit of approximately 500 to 1,000 euros is common, refundable after the boat is returned undamaged.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or an experienced mariner, Reykjavik offers a range of affordable charter options. Browse the latest listings and check availability on BoatHire24, where you can also list your own vessel to reach a worldwide audience. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Reykjavik or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Reykjavik and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
